name: Sue's Swiss Bread
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Unbleached white flour, divided (6 to 8)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch 3/24/97
In a small saucepan over medium heat, heat milk until it steams. Add butter and remove the pan from heat. Let cool to body temperature.
Combine yeast, warm water, and sugar in a large mixing bowl. Cover with a clean dish towel and place the bowl in a warm place for 10 to 20 minutes, or until the yeast foams. Add the cooled milk mixture, salt, and enough flour to form a thick batter. Stir vigorously about 20 times with a wooden spoon.
Cover the bowl again and let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough. Add enough remaining flour by half-cupfuls to make a kneadable dough. Transfer the dough to a floured board or counter; knead for 10 minutes, or until elastic and silky to touch.
Clean the bowl; place the kneaded dough into the bowl. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until the dough is doubled in volume.
Lightly oil a 9-inch-by-13-inch baking sheet. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Divide the dough into 3 strands. Place on the baking sheet and braid as you would a girl's hair, tucking the ends under to finish braiding. Lightly brush the top of the loaf with beaten egg.
Place the loaf in the oven; reduce heat to 375 degrees. Bake for 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until golden brown. Let cool before cutting.
Yield: 1 loaf.
